note: Start the runtime-ownership run by proving current drop-in wire behavior still matches pinned Valkey. This is the compatibility floor for every later perf/architecture packet.
Capture the current pipeline-depth matrix before any runtime-ownership edits. Numbers are evidence, not a claim of parity.
Short stack-sampling run to confirm the post-0004595 hotspot shape before architecture packets fire. Uses smoke-size workloads so the loop can make progress unattended.

Architect-only. Do NOT implement the runtime rewrite. Read latest benchmark/profile evidence, map the faithful Valkey runtime ownership boundary, refine this packet graph if needed, and explicitly mark sharding / benchmark-only bypasses as non-goals. Any implementation packets you create must preserve normal dispatch, ACL, MULTI/EXEC, scripting, expiration, pub/sub, blocking wakeups, AOF, replication, and RDB semantics.

Add a wire-diff corpus script that stresses the semantics most likely to regress under runtime ownership: transaction order, WATCH invalidation, expiration-on-access, pub/sub non-interference if expressible in the corpus, blocking-command edge cases if safe/non-hanging, selected DB behavior, and script/replication-safe command ordering where supported. Do not edit existing corpus files or normalize away differences.

Fix only the CLIENT RESET / CLIENT GETNAME canary divergence. After RESET clears the client name, CLIENT GETNAME must return RESP nil, not an empty bulk string. Do not touch transaction or runtime-owner code. Focused gate: cargo build --bin redis-server && bash harness/oracle/smoke.sh --skip-build.

Fix only the nested MULTI canary divergence. A MULTI issued while already in MULTI must mark the transaction dirty so EXEC returns EXECABORT, matching upstream. Do not alter SELECT or owner-loop behavior in this packet. Focused gate: cargo build --bin redis-server && bash harness/oracle/smoke.sh --skip-build.

Fix only the queued SELECT inside MULTI/EXEC canary divergence. SELECT queued inside EXEC must affect later queued commands and the post-EXEC selected DB without applying at queue time. Preserve normal dispatch and do not implement RuntimeOwner here. If this requires a larger CommandContext redesign, leave a TODO(architect) and fail clearly instead of hacking around the oracle.
Run the full wire-diff corpus after the split runtime-owner canary fixers. If this fails, fix behavior or canary scope before any runtime scaffold work.

Create a typed RuntimeOwner scaffold without changing the default product path. Allowed: new runtime_owner module; types are exactly the rows owned by crates/redis-server/src/runtime_owner.rs in harness/architecture/object-vocabulary.tsv (authoritative — do not re-enumerate here so the two sources cannot drift); and tests proving construction/queue semantics for those types. Allowed in main.rs only: module declaration or clearly disabled wiring. Forbidden: adding any new workspace dep (no mio/polling/tokio/libc-epoll — the poller crate is a TODO(human) decision in harness/architecture/decisions/runtime-ownership.md), benchmark-only GET/SET/PING/INCR path, bypassing dispatch, disabling TLS/pubsub/blocking/replication/AOF/RDB, or making the scaffold default before oracle evidence exists. PollDriverHandle stays abstract (no concrete poller import) until human review. SlotId must be a newtype, not a bare u32 alias.
Compatibility gate after the inert runtime-owner scaffold. The scaffold is only acceptable if normal Valkey behavior remains byte-compatible on the surveyed corpus.
Refresh the profile matrix after the scaffold. Expected result may be neutral; this packet confirms no accidental slowdown and gives the next architect packet real evidence.
Longer sampled run after the safe scaffold. This is the evidence handoff for the next human/architect decision: proceed to real owner-loop migration, take a small allocator/hasher cleanup, or stop.

High-compute architect pass. Read the current code, benchmark/profile evidence, and docs/RUNTIME_OWNER_OVERNIGHT_ARCHITECTURE.md. Do not implement. Either strengthen the std-nonblocking owner-loop packet so it is faithful and bounded, or write a clear blocker. Keep the no benchmark-only bypass rule binding.

Implement the bounded std nonblocking owner-loop experiment locked by harness/architecture/decisions/runtime-ownership.md. Plain TCP only: replace the per-client read/dispatch/writer-thread path with one RuntimeOwner loop over nonblocking TcpListener/TcpStream slots; TLS stays on the existing thread-per-client path. Keep redis_commands::dispatch via CommandContext::with_server and parse_inline_or_multibulk_into; no command-specific fast paths, sharding, new poller dependency, raw poller unsafe, or disabled features. Transitional DB rule: use existing global_databases() Arc<Mutex<RedisDb>> handles as the live DB source, holding selected DB guards only for bounded dispatch batches; do not create a divergent live Vec<RedisDb>. Foreign bytes for pub/sub, blocked wakeups, WAIT/replication, and similar routes must enter through per-slot mpsc receivers drained by the owner loop, not foreign socket writes. Preserve maxclients, client-info, connected-client metrics, selected DB, RESET/QUIT, pubsub/replica/blocked cleanup. If local wire-smoke cannot be restored in the packet, stop with TODO(architect) instead of weakening behavior.
Full wire-smoke after the first plain-TCP owner loop. This must be green before any benchmark number is trusted.
Benchmark the default product path after the owner loop is green. Update evidence only; do not hand-edit benchmark docs unless the runner owns that artifact.
Profile the owner loop after the first real benchmark. The next perf packet must use this evidence, not guesses.

One bounded polish packet after owner-loop profile evidence exists. Improve the measured owner-loop bottleneck without bypassing normal dispatch or command semantics. Candidate areas: write flushing, query-buffer draining, per-tick batching, client slot iteration, or reply buffer movement. Do not add sharding or command-specific fast paths.
Full wire-smoke after owner-loop polish.
Final overnight profile matrix after owner-loop polish.
Final overnight hotspot profile. This is the handoff for the next architecture session.
Start the next performance push by reproving current wire compatibility at HEAD. No benchmark or architecture packet counts unless this stays green.
Fresh profile matrix at current HEAD after the runtime-owner polish. This is the pre-poller baseline for the next push.
Long p100 sampled hotspot run at current HEAD. The following architect packet must use this evidence and not throughput vibes.

Add a typed json_command runner for raw call-tree/flamegraph-style profiling artifacts. On macOS it may use /usr/bin/sample; on Linux it may use perf/cargo-flamegraph if available. It must emit RunnerResult JSON, attach raw artifacts under harness/bench/results or harness/bench/profiles, include hardware/tool metadata, and not change server behavior or benchmark methodology outside this runner.
Run the new call-tree/flamegraph artifact profiler before poller implementation. This should produce raw profiler artifacts plus a small typed summary for the architect packet.

High-compute architect pass for the next wall. Read the current code and all runtime-owner-6 evidence. Either approve a concrete mio-readiness poller packet for plain TCP or block with a better packet graph. Do not implement. Keep normal redis_commands::dispatch, RESP parser, ACL, transactions, scripting, expiration, pub/sub, blocking, AOF, RDB, and replication in scope. No sharding, owner-owned DB migration, TLS migration, or benchmark-only fast paths in this packet family.

Implement the architect-approved mio readiness poller for the default plain-TCP RuntimeOwner path. Add mio as a workspace dependency with os-poll and net features. Replace the std linear accept/read/write slot scan with mio::Poll, mio::Events, and stable slot tokens: listener readiness accepts until WouldBlock; client readability drains socket reads and dispatches parsed commands; writable interest is enabled only while ClientWriteBuffer has pending bytes and removed when empty. Slots that hit MAX_COMMANDS_PER_SLOT_TICK with complete commands still buffered must be rescheduled by the owner loop without waiting for a new socket readiness edge. Keep one synchronous owner thread and keep redis_commands::dispatch via CommandContext::with_server and parse_inline_or_multibulk_into as the only command path. Keep global_databases() as transitional storage; no owner-owned live Vec<RedisDb>. Foreign pub/sub, blocked wakeup, WAIT/replication, and similar bytes stay on per-slot mpsc receivers; drain them after poll returns and on a short bounded timeout, queue owner write interest, and do not let foreign threads write owner sockets. Preserve maxclients, client-info, connected-client metrics, selected DB, RESET/QUIT, pub/sub cleanup, replica cleanup, blocked-key cleanup, ACL, MULTI/EXEC, scripting, expiration, AOF, RDB, and replication semantics. No tokio, polling crate, raw epoll/kqueue/libc poller code, unsafe poller budget increase, sharding, TLS migration, command-specific fast paths, disabled background semantics, or public performance claim. Gate with cargo check/test plus full wire-smoke; if compatibility cannot be restored, stop with TODO(architect) rather than weakening behavior.
Full wire-smoke after the poller owner-loop packet. Benchmarks only count if this passes.
Post-poller profile matrix on the default product path.
Post-poller p100 hotspot profile. Use this to decide whether the next wall is owner-owned DB, parser/serializer, allocation/copy churn, or command dispatch.
Post-poller call-tree/flamegraph artifact run. This should make the next packet choice auditable.
Guard against a large p100 PING throughput-ratio regression after the poller packet. Threshold is configured in harness/runners.toml.
Guard against a large p100 GET throughput-ratio regression after the poller packet. Threshold is configured in harness/runners.toml.
Guard against a large p100 SET throughput-ratio regression after the poller packet. Threshold is configured in harness/runners.toml.
Guard against a large p100 INCR throughput-ratio regression after the poller packet. Threshold is configured in harness/runners.toml.

Manual follow-up only. Use post-poller evidence to scope the full owner-owned DB migration. Do not implement. This packet should decompose selected DB ownership, active expire, AOF, replication, RDB, scripting, blocking, pub/sub, WATCH/MULTI, and TLS interaction before any code packet is allowed.

Reduce avoidable command timing / slowlog predicate overhead shown in post-poller calltree evidence. Upstream still times every command in server.c::call(), so do not disable slowlog, command duration semantics, latency/accounting, AOF argv snapshots, replication propagation, ACL, maxmemory, readonly gates, or normal redis_commands::dispatch. Prefer a cheap live-config predicate/cache or consolidated duration capture. No command-specific PING/GET/SET/INCR fast paths. Local gates: cargo check/tests for touched crates plus full wire-smoke before benchmarks.
Wire-smoke after command timing / slowlog hot-path work. Benchmarks only count if this passes.
Profile matrix after command timing / slowlog hot-path work.
Call-tree evidence after command timing / slowlog hot-path work. The next packet must use this evidence, not assumptions.

Reduce hot reply encoding allocation/copy overhead shown in GET/SET/PING calltrees. Add direct Client/CommandContext helpers for common RESP2 legacy replies where safe: simple string, integer, null bulk, bulk bytes, array header. Preserve generic write_frame/RESP3 native frame behavior, exact wire bytes, error replies, pub/sub push frames, and command APIs. No command-specific benchmark bypass; commands should still call CommandContext reply helpers. Local gates: existing exact reply unit tests plus full wire-smoke.
Wire-smoke after direct reply-buffer hot-path work.
Profile matrix after direct reply-buffer hot-path work.
Call-tree evidence after direct reply-buffer hot-path work. The next packet must inspect whether encode_resp2/RawVec/malloc samples moved.

Add a faithful no-watch fast path for dirty-key signaling. Current SET/INCR calltrees show RedisDb::signal_modified -> watched_keys_touch -> global mutex/allocation even when no clients watch keys. Match upstream touchWatchedKey's early return shape: skip lock/allocation when no watchers exist, but preserve WATCH/MULTI/EXEC dirty-CAS behavior when watchers exist, including selected DB and expired watched-key semantics already covered by canaries. Do not disable WATCH or route around normal RedisDb::set_key/sync_delete signaling. Add or strengthen a wire canary if coverage is missing.
Wire-smoke after WATCH dirty-key fast path. Runtime-owner WATCH canaries must remain green.
Profile matrix after WATCH dirty-key fast path.
Final call-tree evidence after the three small hot-path packets. Use this to decide whether to return to owner-owned DB architecture.

Guard against a large p100 PING throughput-ratio regression after the small hot-path wave. Run these regression packets sequentially or through the scheduler resource lock; runner auto-commits share git state.
Guard against a large p100 GET throughput-ratio regression after the small hot-path wave.
Guard against a large p100 SET throughput-ratio regression after the small hot-path wave.
Guard against a large p100 INCR throughput-ratio regression after the small hot-path wave.

Add owner-compatible selected-DB and cross-DB routing APIs to CommandContext while keeping the current live storage model. Preserve redis_commands::dispatch as the only command path. Existing borrowed-DB constructors may remain for tests and legacy startup/TLS surfaces, but owner-loop dispatch should be able to pass a DB-list route keyed by Client::db_index. SELECT validation must use the configured DB count rather than a hard-coded local assumption where the context can provide it. Do not move the live DB list into RuntimeOwner yet and do not introduce a second live keyspace.
Full wire-smoke after CommandContext DB-routing groundwork. The next cross-DB packet is not trusted unless this passes.

Remove direct global_databases() command access from MOVE, COPY, SWAPDB, queued EXEC selected-DB routing, and blocked wake paths by using the CommandContext/owner DB-list APIs. Preserve Valkey semantics: SELECT is per-client, WATCH invalidation is per selected DB, MOVE/COPY signal the correct DBs, and SWAPDB swaps keyspace contents without swapping watched/blocking/ready-key ownership structures. Add or strengthen runtime-owner canaries for SELECT plus MOVE/COPY/SWAPDB/WATCH interactions. Do not flip RuntimeOwner to own the live Vec<RedisDb> in this packet.
Full wire-smoke after cross-DB owner-routing work, including runtime-owner canaries. The live DB owner must not flip until this passes.

Move the default command-execution keyspace to a RuntimeOwner-owned Vec<RedisDb>. Main/startup may load RDB/AOF into the owner DB vector before the owner loop starts. RuntimeOwner dispatch must use the CommandContext DB-list route and must not use global_databases() as a normal command store. Active expire must run in the owner cron/expire step or through an explicit owner event. TLS command execution, replication apply, runtime AOF replay, and persistence snapshot entry must route through owner-owned DB APIs/channels or stop with TODO(architect)/TODO(human); do not leave any of them mutating a divergent global DB. Preserve redis_commands::dispatch, selected DB, WATCH/MULTI/EXEC, blocking, pub/sub, scripting, ACL, maxmemory, readonly-replica, AOF, replication, RDB, and RESP semantics. No TLS socket migration is required in this packet, but no TLS/global-DB divergence is allowed after the flip.
Full wire-smoke after the owner-owned DB loop flip. Benchmarks only count if this passes.
Profile matrix after RuntimeOwner owns the live DB vector. This is telemetry only, not a public speed claim.
Call-tree evidence after the owner-owned DB loop flip. Confirm normal command execution no longer samples the transitional global DB lock path and identify the next architecture wall.
Guard against a large p100 PING throughput-ratio regression after the owner-owned DB loop flip.
Guard against a large p100 GET throughput-ratio regression after the owner-owned DB loop flip.
Guard against a large p100 SET throughput-ratio regression after the owner-owned DB loop flip.
Guard against a large p100 INCR throughput-ratio regression after the owner-owned DB loop flip.
Telemetry-only survey of currently unswept upstream TCL unit files with per-file timeouts. Generates evidence and packet candidates; does not create a public conformance claim by itself.
